age: Q1. What is your age group?
country: Q3. Which country are you currently residing in?
num_flying: Q4. How many times did you travel by air in the last 12 months? Please count one round trip as one trip.
purpose_flying: Q4.1 When you travel by air, what is the purpose of your trips? Choose all that apply.
change_flying_binary: Q4.2 In the past 3 years, have you made changes to your flying behaviour for environmental reasons?
change_flying_behav: Q4.3 Which changes have you made to your flying behaviour in the past 3 years for environmental reasons? Please choose all that apply.
change_flying_impact: Q4.4 When booking flights, how often do you consider the environmental impact of your choice of flight, for instance, choosing a direct vs. connecting flights or only booking airlines with strong sustainability policies?
wtp_flying_likert: Q4.5 How willing are you to pay an additional fee if it guarantees that the additional global warming from CO₂ caused by your flight is entirely balanced out?
wtp_flying_amount: Q4.6 Imagine a flight costs US$ 100. How much more on top of this would you be willing to pay if it guarantees that the additional global warming from CO₂ caused by your flight is entirely balanced out?
reduce_flight: Q4.7 How willing are you to reduce the number of flights you take each year for environmental reasons?
concern_flying: Q5. How concerned are you about the environmental impacts of air travel (e.g., carbon emissions, noise pollution)?
govspend_flying: Q6. How willing are you to support your government spending public funds to reduce or balance out the additional global warming  from CO₂ caused by my flight?
statements_flying_1: Q7. To what extent do you agree with the following statements regarding the additional warming  from CO₂ caused by a flight? - Additional global warming from CO₂ caused by flying is a serious issue
statements_flying_2: Q7. To what extent do you agree with the following statements regarding the additional warming  from CO₂ caused by a flight? - There is enough proof that flying affects the climate
statements_flying_3: Q7. To what extent do you agree with the following statements regarding the additional warming  from CO₂ caused by a flight? - Additional global warming from CO₂ caused by flying is not well understood, and worries about them are mostly guesses
terms_heard: Q8. Which of the following terms have you heard before? Choose all that apply.
know_co2na:
Q9. How much do you know about CO₂-neutral aviation? 
Note: CO₂-neutral aviation means the concept of preventing aviation CO₂ emissions from contributing to further global warming.
means_know_co2na: Q10. How did you come to know about CO₂-neutral aviation? Select all that applies. - Selected Choice
means_know_co2na_12_TEXT: Q10. How did you come to know about CO₂-neutral aviation? Select all that applies. - Others (please specify) - Text

realistic_co2na: Q12. Do you believe achieving CO₂-neutral aviation by 2050 is realistic?
efforts_co2na_1: Q13. How do you view the below efforts toward CO₂-neutral aviation? - Reducing CO₂ emissions from flying is very important for today's world and we cannot ignore it
efforts_co2na_2: Q13. How do you view the below efforts toward CO₂-neutral aviation? - Cutting CO₂ emissions from flying will bring real benefits to people and communities
efforts_co2na_3: Q13. How do you view the below efforts toward CO₂-neutral aviation? - Humanity has a moral responsibility to work towards CO₂-neutral aviation
important_co2na: Q14. In your opinion, how important is it for aviation to reduce its CO₂ emissions to achieve neutrality by 2050?
publicinput_co2na: Q15. To what extent do you agree that the general public’s input to decision making is key to helping aviation reach CO₂-neutrality?
trap2: Q16. To move forward, please select “Not at all”.
pay_support_co2na_1: Q17. To what extent do you agree with the following. I would pay more or support actions to help aviation reach CO₂-neutral aviation if: - Airlines clearly explained how they are cutting CO₂ emissions and report their progress
pay_support_co2na_2: Q17. To what extent do you agree with the following. I would pay more or support actions to help aviation reach CO₂-neutral aviation if: - The government are more involved in decisions about flying, like setting fuel rules, taxes or limits on flights
pay_support_co2na_3: Q17. To what extent do you agree with the following. I would pay more or support actions to help aviation reach CO₂-neutral aviation if: - Airlines are more open about how extra charges for sustainable fuels are spent
pay_support_co2na_4: Q17. To what extent do you agree with the following. I would pay more or support actions to help aviation reach CO₂-neutral aviation if: - The extra costs of sustainable fuels are fair and clearly help the environment
take_actions_co2na_1: Q18. How willing would you be to doing the following to contribute towards achieving CO₂-neutral aviation? - Read up about airline's climate actions and pick airlines that do more for the climate
take_actions_co2na_2: Q18. How willing would you be to doing the following to contribute towards achieving CO₂-neutral aviation? - Speak up on social media or in social groups for airlines that aim for CO₂-neutrality
pref_path_co2na_1: Q19. To what extent do you agree with the following statements about how CO₂-neutral aviation could be achieved? CO₂-neutral aviation can be achieved when: - Airplanes keep flying as usual but balance out their CO₂ emissions by reducing or removing the same amount of CO₂ somewhere else
pref_path_co2na_2: Q19. To what extent do you agree with the following statements about how CO₂-neutral aviation could be achieved? CO₂-neutral aviation can be achieved when: - Airplanes use cleaner fuels made from plants, without changing the planes themselves
pref_path_co2na_3: Q19. To what extent do you agree with the following statements about how CO₂-neutral aviation could be achieved? CO₂-neutral aviation can be achieved when: - Airplanes use new types of fuels made from clean electricity, without changing the planes themselves
pref_path_co2na_4: Q19. To what extent do you agree with the following statements about how CO₂-neutral aviation could be achieved? CO₂-neutral aviation can be achieved when: - New types of airplanes are powered with clean electricity to replace old ones
pref_path_co2na_5: Q19. To what extent do you agree with the following statements about how CO₂-neutral aviation could be achieved? CO₂-neutral aviation can be achieved when: - Airplanes use less fuel by being lighter, choosing better routes and better managing air traffic
pref_path_co2na_6: Q19. To what extent do you agree with the following statements about how CO₂-neutral aviation could be achieved? CO₂-neutral aviation can be achieved when: - Airplanes still emit CO₂ to the atmosphere, but the same amount is separately removed from the air elsewhere and stored permanently
measure_achiev_co2na_1: Q20. Assuming each option costs the same, to what extent do you support the following measures of achieving CO₂-neutral aviation? - Fuel for airplanes are made from plants instead of regular fossil fuel
measure_achiev_co2na_2: Q20. Assuming each option costs the same, to what extent do you support the following measures of achieving CO₂-neutral aviation? - Fuel for airplanes are made from clean electricity
measure_achiev_co2na_3: Q20. Assuming each option costs the same, to what extent do you support the following measures of achieving CO₂-neutral aviation? - Airplanes are fully electric
measure_achiev_co2na_4: Q20. Assuming each option costs the same, to what extent do you support the following measures of achieving CO₂-neutral aviation? - Planting trees or protecting forests to balance out CO₂ emitted by airplanes
measure_achiev_co2na_5: Q20. Assuming each option costs the same, to what extent do you support the following measures of achieving CO₂-neutral aviation? - Removing CO₂ out of the air and storing it permanently in rock deep underground to balance out CO₂ emitted by airplanes
